title: Leverage Points

content.statement: Small moves in the right place create massive change.
content.question: What's the one leverage point you're ignoring because it feels too simple?
content.business_lens: Pricing change > more content. Offer > traffic.
sop: List every lever available to you right now: pricing, offer, positioning, one key hire, one process.
Rate the Change magnitude and Leverage Multiplier for each 1-10 (see calculation, page 5).
Pick the single highest-scoring lever and ignore the rest for the next 2 weeks.
Make the change and measure downstream impact across multiple metrics, not just one.
Move to the next-highest leverage point only after this one is fully implemented.
questions: What's the one leverage point you're ignoring because it feels too simple?
If you could only change one variable in your business this month, which one touches the most other numbers?
What's something you've been working hard on that, honestly, barely moves anything else downstream?

definitions.def: A small intervention that produces an outsized effect.

content.equation_example: Change magnitude (a single pricing adjustment) rated 4, Leverage Multiplier 8 (this one change touches 8 downstream numbers, revenue, margin, positioning). Score = 4 times 8 = 32. A higher multiplier means the same-sized change produces outsized results because of where it sits in the system; a high-effort change with a low multiplier of 1-2 means you're working hard in a low-leverage spot.
content.closing_statement: The smallest lever in the right spot moves the most.

caption:
Principle Nº 109: Leverage Points

Small moves in the right place create massive change.

Ask yourself: What's the one leverage point you're ignoring because it feels too simple?
